behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is not just routing; it requires correct explicit-content detection by the vision model. REAL-INFRA is not the core boundary. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model judgment of image content; stubbed labels would always pass and hide misclassification. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agent chain required. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss real misclassification drift/calibration errors. Integration would miss failures where real model output differs from fixture labels on difficult explicit images. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text describes one model decision plus hold/publish action, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated set of real explicit images through the production-like moderation call path (real vision model enabled) and assert each is held-for-review and not published. Include near-threshold examples to catch false negatives that mocks cannot expose.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior targets semantic robustness against obfuscation, which is model-quality dependent. REAL-INFRA is secondary. LLM-DEP: real text-model behavior is required; deterministic fixtures cannot validate robustness to unicode/leet variants. MULTI-STEP: single classification decision, so not Workflow. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss tokenizer/embedding/model handling of obfuscated forms. Integration would miss real-model misses on adversarial variants because stubs return expected flags. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text does not require chained agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run adversarial hate-speech corpus (leet, homoglyphs, spacing/punctuation tricks) through real text moderation model and assert flagged+held outcomes across variants of same phrase.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure appears at module handoff/orchestration (image result + text result -> decision -> publish path). Unit on decision helper alone can miss wiring bugs causing drop/hold despite pass results. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess for this behavior if both model outputs are controlled as pass. (b) correctness here does not depend on model quality/content, only pass statuses. (c) no required multi-agentic chain for the assertion. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit misses queue/repo/publisher interaction bugs (e.g., event not emitted). Above-Integration miss-proof not strong: System would not add unique failure boundary for this specific logic path.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process test wiring of moderation orchestrator + persistence/publish modules with model adapters stubbed to 'pass'. Assert final state is published, not held, and no silent drop (record exists + publish event emitted).

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: latency target concerns end-to-end pipeline under realistic contention; isolated function timing is insufficient. REAL-INFRA: real network/queue/runtime effects matter. LLM-DEP: path includes real AI model calls whose latency/distribution affects SLA. MULTI-STEP: explicitly sequences multiple agentic steps (vision then text then decision), so Workflow. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit misses queueing/backpressure and cross-stage latency accumulation. Integration misses real API/network latency variance and concurrency bottlenecks hidden by local stubs. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected as final because behavior text explicitly requires full multi-step pipeline timing, not a single model step.
behaviors.test_description: Execute load test at peak-like throughput against production-like deployment with real vision+text models enabled; measure submission-to-final-decision latency and assert p95/p99 (or strict max per spec) stays within 5s for accepted sample window.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure often appears with real outage signals (timeouts, DNS/TLS errors, 5xx bursts, connection resets) and retry queue integration. REAL-INFRA: yes, real HTTP/network/subprocess behavior to model service boundary is central. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output quality; this is failure-handling infrastructure. MULTI-STEP: not agentic-sequencing dependent. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit misses real exception mapping and retry enqueue side effects. Integration misses transport-layer failures that stubs do not emulate faithfully, causing false confidence on fallback path. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text is about unavailability handling, not model judgment correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real unavailability of vision service in a system test (e.g., blackhole endpoint or terminate service) and submit image posts; assert posts enter retry queue with retry metadata and are not auto-approved/published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: consistency for borderline content depends on real model stochasticity/calibration and any stabilization logic. REAL-INFRA may contribute but is not primary boundary. LLM-DEP: yes, must observe real model outputs across repeated submissions; stubs force artificial consistency and hide drift/flakiness. MULTI-STEP: repeated calls, but not a distinct multi-stage agent pipeline requirement. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit misses real nondeterministic classification variance. Integration misses true model randomness/temperature/service-version effects masked by fixed fixtures. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text focuses on consistency of one model decision type over repeats, not multi-agent handoff pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the identical borderline image N times within the target window using the real vision model and assert decision consistency threshold (e.g., same outcome in >=X% or strict identical per spec), while recording model/version metadata.
